title: Vi Vision Selector Archetype (render-type)
description:
The SELECTOR archetype — an instance of archetype.schema.json (render_kind='selector'): renders a CHOICE among options/modes/things and DRIVES the chosen value. Tim's 'selectors'.

WHAT IT RENDERS: a set of choices (each with legibility name/is — meaning, never a machine-name) + the CURRENT selection, as a navigable pick surface (not a raw dropdown). Picking writes/sets the value (the take). Grounded in REAL usage — the mode-selector (pilot/inspect/dev), tool-picking (FACE-3 — choose a tool to run), option-choosing, any 'pick one (or some)' surface. DNA renders (lyRender); the keystone hosts; the resolver places it across the device axis.

THE TAKE: select → set-value. from_slot='options' (the operator acts on the choices); writes the chosen value (a set/select mark or a bound variable). A read-only selector (no take) is a legend/key.

LAWS (inherited from archetype.schema): operator-legibility (the choices read as MEANING + their implication, never bare ids — touch-visible) · DNA-token-built · resolver-placed · navigable-not-text · native dual-device.

options.description: The CHOICES source — a field/resolve giving the selectable things (each carrying legibility name/is + an implication, so the choice reads as meaning).

current.description: The CURRENT selection source — a field-path or 'resolve:state' (the selected value, so the surface shows what's chosen).

groups.description: Optional: a grouping source (choices clustered by a dimension) — derived, never hardcoded group names.
